title: Wildfire Smoke PM2.5 Exposure And Respiratory Health Impact In Fire-Prone Regions
summary: Build a verified workflow for estimating wildfire-smoke-specific PM2.5 exposure and respiratory health impact in fire-prone regions, linking satellite-derived smoke plumes, chemical-transport model composition, ground-monitor PM2.5, and health-utilization data with explicit exposure-validation methodology and non-smoke PM2.5 confound controls.
why_it_matters: Wildfire smoke contributes up to 25 percent of global ambient PM2.5, and the fraction is rising as climate change extends fire seasons across boreal, temperate, and tropical regions. Smoke-derived PM2.5 may be 2-3 times more toxic per unit mass than other PM2.5 sources due to oxidative potential and co-pollutant profiles, though this differential toxicity finding requires further replication. Health systems in fire-prone regions need verified smoke-specific exposure estimates to target advisories and resource allocation without misattributing non-smoke respiratory burden.

success_metrics.target: Every smoke-exposure estimate links satellite plume data, model composition, and ground-monitor validation with documented correlation and gap analysis.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation, evidence.json completeness check, and reviewer spot-checks against satellite-to-monitor validation methodology.

success_metrics.target: No smoke-toxicity claim is merged without documenting the replication status, confounding structure, and fire-regime-specific limitations of the differential toxicity finding.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms every toxicity claim has explicit uncertainty bounds and non-replicated status documented.

success_metrics.target: Every regional health-impact claim documents whether ground-monitor and health-outcome data is available and sufficient for the claimed geographic scope.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms evidence-gap documentation for every region where health-impact claims are made, with explicit statement where LMIC data is absent.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, exposure-validation safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
